codes_manifest.display: Acute arterial occlusion of upper limb
Embolism and thrombosis of arteries of upper extremities

transform_flags: Age (68) and sex (male) are NOT stated in the source note — inferred as an older adult with atrial fibrillation; please confirm on clinician review.
Provisional case_id SPEC-VASC-01-00208 — SEQ is provisional; ingest --reseq / maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-VASC-01 bucket.
Specialty coded VASC (Vascular Surgery); reclassify to EMG (Emergency Medicine) if the reviewer prefers an undifferentiated-emergency framing.

Telehealth reprojection applied: clinician-only findings (absent radial/ulnar/brachial pulses, temperature-demarcation line, collapsed veins, AF on auscultation, in-person vitals BP 145/90 HR 115 irregular SpO2 98%) sealed into 10/11 with telehealth_limits; only patient-observable/reportable findings placed in 01.objective_data_offered.

In-person interventions reprojected: clinician-administered oral oxycodone and the 000 call become the AI-appropriate action of recognise + escalate 000 + positioning advice. Oxycodone recorded as should_NOT_recommend and apixaban catch-up dosing as not_indicated_here — no dose synthesised for the AI Doctor.
All SNOMED CT-AU / ICD-10-AM / AMT codes are unverified candidates pending terminology receipt.

correct_baseline_tier set to T5 (000 ambulance) given an immediately-threatened limb plus a patient who lives alone and cannot self-transport; minimum_viable_tier_for_pass set to T4 (urgent ED) as the minimally acceptable recognition of the emergency.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
